body:
## Summary

Fixes the only hard failure in CI/CD run [27703726124](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/actions/runs/27703726124): the **Deploy Demo to GitHub Pages** job crashed with `System.IO.IOException: No space left on device`.

### Root cause
The `deploy-demo` job restored a **shared, multi-gigabyte `target/` cache** keyed `${{ runner.os }}-cargo-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}` — the *same* key the `lint` job (`cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`) and `test` job populate. On top of that restored tree the job built the web vendor bundle, ran `cargo doc`, duplicated `target/doc` into the Pages artifact, and tarred it — exhausting the `ubuntu-latest` runner's ~14 GB root disk. The runner worker process itself then crashed while writing its diagnostic log.

This was a **self-introduced regression**: PR #479 added the `cargo doc` step + `target` cache to `deploy-demo`. The upstream [rust pipeline template](https://github.com/link-foundation/rust-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template)'s doc-deploy job caches **no** `target/` and is unaffected — so there is **no upstream bug to file** (full template comparison in the case study).

### Fix (`.github/workflows/release.yml`, `deploy-demo` job)
- **Cache only `~/.cargo/registry` + `~/.cargo/git`** under a dedicated `*-cargo-docs-*` key — the bloated `target/` tree is never restored here. `cargo doc --no-deps --lib` rebuilds its own small `target/` from the cached registry.
- **Free unused pre-installed SDKs** (dotnet / android / ghc / codeql) and prune docker images before the heavy build (~20–30 GB reclaimed).
- **Log `df -h`** before/after cleanup so disk pressure is visible on every run (verbose/debug for future iterations).

### Other CI signals (triaged, no code change)
- ⚠️ *Node.js 20 deprecated … `actions/github-script@60a0d83…`* — transitive inside `codecov/codecov-action@v5`; not referenced by our workflows. External, non-blocking.
- ⚠️ *Rust file approaching 1000-line limit* (×10) — intended advisory from the repo's own file-size lint, not a false positive.

### Case study
Full evidence + analysis (timeline, requirements, root causes, solutions, template comparison) in [`docs/case-studies/issue-523/`](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/tree/issue-523-d811f560cba2/docs/case-studies/issue-523).

## Verification
- `release.yml` parses cleanly (`yaml.safe_load`).
- Deploy cache no longer lists `target` and uses an isolated key → cannot restore the multi-GB lint/test tree.
- Final proof: the next green **Deploy Demo to GitHub Pages** run on `main`.

Fixes #523

Adding .gitkeep for PR creation (default mode).
This file will be removed when the task is complete.

Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/523

…#523)

The Deploy Demo to GitHub Pages job crashed with 'No space left on device'.
It restored the multi-gigabyte target/ cache shared with the lint/test jobs
(lint runs cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features), then layered the web
bundle, cargo doc, the docs copy and the Pages artifact on top until the
ubuntu-latest runner's ~14 GB root disk filled and the runner worker crashed.

- Cache only ~/.cargo/registry and ~/.cargo/git under a dedicated
  *-cargo-docs-* key so the bloated target/ tree is never restored here
  (matches the rust pipeline template's no-target-cache doc deploy).
- Free unused pre-installed SDKs (dotnet/android/ghc/codeql) and prune
  docker images before the heavy build for headroom.
- Log df -h before/after cleanup for future disk diagnosis.
- Add docs/case-studies/issue-523 with full evidence and analysis.
- Add changelog fragment.
commits.messageHeadline: fix(ci): stop deploy-demo from restoring bloated target cache (issue …

title: fix(ci): resolve 'No space left on device' in Deploy Demo job (issue #523)
